steps.title: Use favorable over total
steps.content: The favorable outcome is choosing a child who picked a vegetarian sandwich. The table shows $9$ vegetarian choices out of $50$ children total.

steps.title: Write the probability
steps.content: $$P(\text{vegetarian})=\frac{9}{50}$$ No percent conversion is needed because the choices are fractions.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• $\frac{9}{100}$, uses the wrong total.
  • $\frac{9}{50}$, uses $9$ vegetarian choices out of $50$ children. Correct.
  • $\frac{1}{4}$, treats the four sandwich types as equally chosen, which the table does not show.
  • $\frac{9}{10}$, is far too large for $9$ out of $50$.
That matches the correct answer, B.
